howItWorks.intro: This wavelength & frequency calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: c = λ × f (speed of light = wavelength × frequency)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the relationship between wavelength and frequency?
faq.answer: They are inversely proportional: as wavelength increases, frequency decreases. Their product always equals the speed of light (c = λf). Visible light ranges from 380 nm (violet, high frequency) to 700 nm (red, low frequency).

faq.question: What are the visible light wavelengths?
faq.answer: Violet: 380-450 nm. Blue: 450-495 nm. Green: 495-570 nm. Yellow: 570-590 nm. Orange: 590-620 nm. Red: 620-700 nm. Below 380 nm is ultraviolet, above 700 nm is infrared.

faq.question: What does photon energy mean?
faq.answer: Each photon carries energy proportional to its frequency (E = hf). Higher frequency light (blue, UV) has more energetic photons. This is why UV light causes sunburn but visible light doesn't — UV photons have enough energy to damage DNA.
